title: Skatteprogressivitet: behavioural microsimulator of Swedish tax progressivity
description: Skatteprogressivitet is a static and behavioural microsimulator for the Swedish tax-and-transfer system. It computes effective marginal and average tax rates across the income distribution, decomposes progressivity through the Kakwani, Suits, and residual-progression indices with bootstrap and influence-function standard errors, and evaluates policy counterfactuals. Behavioural responses are modelled through an elasticity-of-taxable-income (ETI) module calibrated against quasi-experimental estimates from the Swedish reform literature.

creators.name: Laitinen-Fredriksson Lundström Imanov, Gustav Olaf Yunus

notes: This software contains no personal data and no real microdata. Synthetic fixtures are generated from a deterministic seed. This work was supported by the Department of Economics, Stockholm University.
